When the sky is rent asunder
and hearkens to the commandFootnote-1 of its Lord, doing what it should;
and when the earth is stretched outFootnote-2
and casts out what is within it and is emptied,Footnote-3
and hearkens to the command of its Lord, doing what it should.Footnote-4
O man, you are striving unto your LordFootnote-5 and you will meet Him.
Whoever is given the Record in his right hand
shall be called to an easy accounting,Footnote-6
and shall return to his people joyfully.Footnote-7
But he who is given the Record behind his back,Footnote-8
shall cry for “perdition,”
and will enter the Blazing Fire.
He used to live joyfully among his people,Footnote-9
thinking he would never revert (to Us).
But no; (how would he not revert)? His Lord was ever watching him.Footnote-10
Nay; I swear by the twilight;
and by the night and what it enfolds,
and by the moon, when it reaches its fullness:
you shall proceed onwards from stage to stage.Footnote-11
So, what is the matter with them that they do not believe,
and when the Qur'an is recited to them, they do not prostrateFootnote-12 themselves?
Instead, the unbelievers reject it, calling it a lie.
Allah knows best what they are accumulating (in their Record).Footnote-13
So give them the good news of a painful chastisement,
except for those who believe and do good deeds. Theirs shall be an unending reward.
